data.title: Inuvo reaffirms Q2 revenue growth view of no less than 25% Y/Y
data.description:
• Inuvo (OTCQB:INVU) has announced that it was reaffirming its prior guidance that the Co. was expecting revenue growth for the second quarter of not less than 25% on a Y/Y basis.
  • The Co. also completed a 1-for-10 reverse stock split of its outstanding common stock, as approved by shareholders at the Annual Meeting held on May 22, 2025.
  • Inuvo’s purpose in effectuating the reverse stock split is to improve the marketability and liquidity of its stock, aiming to attract a broader range of institutional investors and analysts in support of its long-term growth strategy.

data.title: Jeffs' Brands announces 1-for-17 reverse stock split; shares down over 30%
data.description:
• Jeffs' Brands (NASDAQ:JFBR) intends to effect a 1-for-17 reverse stock split.
  • The ordinary shares are expected to begin trading on Nasdaq a post-split basis at the opening of trading on June 16, 2025.
  • The reverse share split will reduce the number of outstanding shares from 9.41M shares to ~553,385 shares.
  • JFBR shares tumble over 30% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Saratoga Investment declares $0.25 dividend
data.description:

  • Payable Sept 24; for shareholders of record Sept 4; ex-div Sept 4.
  • See SAR Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Sterling Infrastructure announces CFO appointment
data.description:
• Sterling Infrastructure (NASDAQ:STRL) announced the appointment of Nicholas Grindstaff to the role of chief financial officer (CFO), effective July 10, 2025.
  • Grindstaff is succeeding Ron Ballschmiede, who served as executive vice president, CFO from November 2015 through May 2024, and as Interim CFO from March 2025.
  • Grindstaff most recently served as CFO of Cinterra.
  • STRL shares fall 1.5% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: MFA Financial declares $0.36 dividend
data.description:

  • Forward yield 14.94%
  • Payable July 31; for shareholders of record June 30; ex-div June 30.
  • See MFA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: IMAX announces $100M increase in share repurchase program
data.description:
• IMAX Corporation (NYSE:IMAX) announced an increase of $100M in its share repurchase program along with a one-year extension through June 30, 2027.
  • This expansion brings IMAX’s total share repurchase authorization to $500M, of which ~$250M remains available.
  • IMAX has repurchased a total of 15.1M of its common shares for an aggregate purchase price of $249.3M pursuant to the current share-repurchase program since it commenced on July 1, 2017.
  • IMAX shares up 1% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Stock index futures cut some losses, bonds rally after soft producer inflation data
data.description:
Stock index futures pared some losses on Thursday, helped by a benign producer inflation report which also sparked a rally in bonds. However, overall sentiment was weighed down by geopolitics and trade tensions.   

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.2%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.4%.

The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics reported a +0.1% M/M rise in the producer price index (PPI) for May vs. +0.2% expected and -0.2% in April. The core PPI also rose +0.1% vs. +0.3% consensus and -0.1% in April. 

U.S. Treasury yields moved sharply lower after the PPI report, as traders snapped up bonds. The 10-year yield (US10Y) fell 6 basis points to 4.36%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) also fell 6 basis points to 3.90%.

Despite the soft PPI data, headlines on Thursday were dominated by rising geopolitical risks in the Middle East, a renewed tariff threat by U.S. President Donald Trump, and a crash of an Air India Boeing (BA) aircraft.    

Trump said he intended to send letters to trading partners in the next week or two setting unilateral tariff rates, ahead of a July 9 deadline to reimpose higher duties on dozens of countries.

"Trump said they would send letters to U.S. trading partners, detailing the threatened 9 July unilateral trade taxes. Market reactions have been a muted negative; investors have become conditioned to expect retreats from such threats. However, the uncertainty created may weigh on US investment and hiring in the near term," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Traders also continued to await any details on the U.S.-China trade deal. 

On geopolitical news, the U.S. government on Wednesday ordered the departure of embassy staff from Baghdad after Iran threatened to attack U.S. bases if talks over its nuclear program fall through.

"Sentiment wasn’t helped by various headlines, and CBS News reported that part of the reason for the U.S. moving staff out of the Middle East was because U.S. officials had been told that Israel was ready to launch an operation into Iran," Reid said.

The Fed balance sheet will come in later in the day.

data.title: A.I. Joe: Mattel recruits OpenAI to design toys
data.description:
Mattel (NASDAQ:MAT) will start using artificial intelligence to design the next generation of toys and even movies through a deal with OpenAI, Bloomberg reported on Thursday.

“Leveraging this incredible technology is going to allow us to really reimagine the future of play,” said Mattel’s (NASDAQ:MAT) chief franchise officer, Josh Silverman.

By employing AI tools, Mattel (NASDAQ:MAT) can create new toys based on its existing brands, personal assistants based on its current stable of characters, and even make existing toys more interactive.

The company will continue to retain all intellectual property and remains in full control of the products it creates through AI, Silverman added. The first release won’t be announced until later this year.

Mattel's (MAT) partnership with OpenAI could accelerate its shift toward creating movies, TV shows, and interactive games based on iconic brands like Barbie, GI Joe, and Barney. The recent launch of Mattel Studios underscores this strategic pivot, aiming to bring its franchises to both the big and small screens.

For OpenAI, the deal with Mattel (MAT) introduces a new avenue for the adoption its artificial intelligence tools. The company has been courting creative outlets for its new AI video generator in meetings across Los Angeles, demonstrating the capabilities of its Sora model which can generate short video clips based on user prompts, as well as extend existing short videos.

But Mattel (MAT) is not the first to incorporate AI into its creative process. Smaller toy manufacturers are already employing AI tools to assist in concept art, as well as the workflow of production. The technology has already been incorporated into logistics, as well as voice-recognition for popular toys like Barbie and in the design of more visually appealing packaging.

data.title: K Wave Media unveils upcoming content lineup, expands Bitcoin strategic reserve plan
data.description:
• K Wave Media (NASDAQ:KWM) on Thursday said that it continues to execute its Bitcoin (BTC-USD) strategic reserve plan, reinforcing its unique hybrid growth and financial strategy.

  • Beginning this summer, KWM will release a diverse lineup targeting both domestic and international audiences via Netflix (NFLX), major broadcasters, and distributors.

  • “Through our diversified content pipeline, we aim to deepen global audience engagement while building long-term investor value,” said a KWM (NASDAQ:KWM) executive. 

  • KWM (KWM) also announced plans to integrate Bitcoin and approved digital currencies as payment options for its content platforms, merchandise, and K-pop investments.

  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Oil tanker stocks on watch due to Mideast warnings, signs of potential attack on Iran
data.description:
Some of the world’s largest ocean tanker operators said they remained calm but cautious after risk warnings from naval forces for vessels operating in and around the Persian Gulf, Bloomberg reported Thursday.

A rare and urgent joint advisory issued by United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations, Maritime Security Center - Horn of Africa, and Joint Maritime Information Center has set off alarm bells for ship operators passing through the Middle East.

President Trump reaffirmed his stance that military action remains on the table if an agreement on Iran's nuclear ambitions is not reached, and Iran threatened to strike U.S. bases in the region if it is attacked.

But officials at four unnamed tanker firms, whose fleets can collectively move nearly 300M barrels of oil and fuel, told Bloomberg that little movement in rates has occurred since the maritime warnings were issued and their companies are not yet making any major adjustments to their operations.

The Strait of Hormuz is a vital chokepoint for global oil markets, as ~25% of the world's oil supplies pass through it; oil prices soared nearly 5% on Wednesday.

Shipping industry trade group BIMCO said a full blown armed conflict - with Israel and the U.S. on one side, and Iran on the other - would most certainly "effectively close the Straits of Hormuz at least for a period of time and drive up oil prices."

Among several potentially relevant stocks: Frontline (NYSE:FRO), Nordic American Tankers (NYSE:NAT), International Seaways (INSW), DHT Holdings (DHT), Scorpio Tankers (STNG), Teekay (TK), Danaos (DAC), Tsakos Energy Navigation (TEN), Ardmore Shipping (ASC), Torm (TRMD), Hafnia (HAFN).
